title: Mammillaria standleyi
intro: Mammillaria standleyi, also known as the Standley's pincushion cactus, is a species of flowering cactus in the genus Mammillaria. This plant is prized for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a valuable remedy for wound healing and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Mammillaria standleyi include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are teas and infusions, as well as tinctures and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Mammillaria standleyi is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is classified under the subfamily Cactoideae and the genus Mammillaria. The specific epithet 'standleyi' is likely named after a botanist or plant explorer who studied this species. The classification is based on its physical characteristics, growth habits, and floral morphology. Mammillaria standleyi is native to Central America, where it grows in a variety of habitats.
